message:
feat(raptorq): add dense Gaussian-elimination fallback for corrupt decoder output

When the fast InactivationDecoder produces CorruptDecodedOutput, fall
back to a dense O(L^3) Gaussian-elimination solver instead of failing:

- Clone symbols before passing to decoder.decode() so they remain
  available for the fallback path
- Add try_reconstruct_dense() method that builds the full L x L
  constraint matrix, substitutes repair equations for missing source
  positions, solves via the same dense solver used by the encoder,
  and reconstructs the payload from the first K intermediate symbols
- This handles edge cases where the sparse solver's intermediate
  representation produces incorrect output, at the cost of higher
  latency only on the fallback path

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
